activation_results.reason: Input restates the canonical use case.
SSH configuration is unrelated to knowledge graphs.
Meta question about skills, not a request to build a knowledge graph.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: Artefact uses 'specialisations' (Consultant properties); no US spellings detected in narrative text.

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: "Knowledge Graph Specification — [Busines"
criterion_results.evidence: Top heading is '# Knowledge Graph: Apex Strategy Partners' rather than the required 'Knowledge Graph Specification — [Business...]' canonical form.

judge_result.notes: The artefact passes the spellings criterion (uses 'specialisations' and otherwise neutral terms throughout). It fails the canonical-section criterion because the document title is 'Knowledge Graph: Apex Strategy Partners' instead of the expected 'Knowledge Graph Specification — [Business...]' header. Substantively the artefact does deliver the intended content — node types, edge types, graph statistics, a sample subgraph, JSON-LD representation, and a PostgreSQL JSONB implementation covering all three target stacks — but the literal canonical-section string is absent, so the overall verdict is partial.
